expectations.text: Flags query-optimization ticket as Misleading Story / Solution Trap
expectations.evidence: Story 1 carries the explicit flag: '⚠ **Misleading Story (Solution Trap)** — "database query optimization" is a prescribed implementation, not a user need.' What-Changed also calls it 'a classic Solution Trap.'

Story 2 carries: '⚠ **Dependency-Locked Story** — the deliverable requires VendorX's encoding integration, which sits outside the team's Zone of Control. Recommend splitting: keep "Upload 4K videos to courses" as the user-facing story, and track "Integrate VendorX encoding pipeline" separately...'

expectations.text: Treats push-notification ticket as clean (no anti-pattern warnings)
expectations.evidence: Story 3: 'This is the cleanest of the three — no anti-pattern warning.' Summary also confirms: 'Story 3 (push notifications): Clean.'

expectations.text: Uses 'I can' throughout; no 'I want' remaining
expectations.evidence: Every narrative uses 'I can' ('I can run the monthly reports...', 'I can upload 4K videos...', 'I can receive a push notification...'). No 'I want' appears.

Each story starts with '**Title:**' (e.g., '**Title:** Faster monthly reports from the reporting service', '**Title:** Upload 4K videos to courses', '**Title:** Notify me when my enrolled course has a new lesson').

expectations.text: Every story's INVEST check lists each of the six principles (I, N, V, E, S, T) individually with its own verdict — no compact 'all criteria met' one-liner, applied consistently to all three stories
expectations.evidence: All three stories list each of I, N, V, E, S, T individually with a verdict. Story 1 has varied verdicts (N-fail, E-fail, T-fail, S-unknown); Story 2 has I-fail, E-partial, S-unknown; Story 3 is all pass — each principle enumerated separately in every story.

expectations.text: Does not add Acceptance Criteria (no AC section or bullet list of measurable requirements) — ACs are out of scope
expectations.evidence: No AC section or AC bullet list. The only AC reference is a brief forward-looking note on Story 3 ('push is the only channel... both are natural follow-ups but not needed in the story itself'), which is allowed.
